commit.message:
Minecart boats (#107)

* docs(ai-team): minecart-boats design review decisions

Session: 2026-02-27-minecart-boats-design-review
Requested by: Jeffrey T. Fritz

Changes:
- merged design review decisions from inbox into decisions.md
- propagated updates to affected agent history files

* fix: ErrorBoatService spawns boats in actual canal water behind buildings

Fix three critical bugs that made error boats non-functional:

1. GetCanalEntrance coordinates: Changed from (ox-2, CanalY, oz+StructureSize/2)
   to (ox+StructureSize, CanalY, oz+StructureSize+4) matching where CanalService
   actually builds per-building E-W canals behind buildings.

2. Boat propulsion: Added {Motion:[-0.5,0.0,0.0]} NBT to summon command giving
   boats westward velocity toward the trunk canal on blue ice.

3. CanalService dependency: Injected nullable CanalService and gated spawning
   on CanalPositions.Count > 0 so boats never spawn before canals exist.

4. Spawn Y: Now uses CanalY (water level) from GetCanalEntrance instead of
   SurfaceY (grass level).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add Aspire dashboard 'Trigger Error' command for API service

Add a dashboard button that flips the API service health check to unhealthy,
triggering the Minecraft worker's error boat detection pipeline:

- API: POST /trigger-error endpoint flips health flag to unhealthy (503)
- AppHost: WithHttpHealthCheck + WithHttpCommand wires dashboard button
  with ErrorCircle icon and confirmation dialog
- Skill doc for team reuse of WithHttpCommand pattern

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(ai-team): merge error boat and dashboard command decisions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: buffer error boat spawns until canals are ready

ErrorBoatService now buffers Unhealthy transitions that arrive before
CanalService finishes building. After canal initialization, Program.cs
triggers a replay of buffered changes so boats spawn correctly.

Fixes the race condition where clicking 'Trigger Error' early in the
worker lifecycle produced no boat because the change event was consumed
and lost before canals existed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Created session log: 2026-02-27-error-boat-buffering.md
- Merged inbox decision: ErrorBoatService buffering pattern
- Propagated team update to Rocket's history
- No deduplication needed (no exact duplicate headings found)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Scribe: Summarize Rocket's history, archive old milestones

- Created history-archive.md with consolidated Milestones 1-4 (2026-02-10 to 2026-02-14)
- Reduced Rocket's main history.md from 82.2KB to ~47KB

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add health check path support for resource monitoring

WithMonitoredResource now detects HealthCheckAnnotation and passes
ASPIRE_RESOURCE_{NAME}_HEALTH_PATH env var to the worker.
AspireResourceMonitor appends it to the poll URL for proper health
detection. API root endpoint also returns 503 when unhealthy.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: trigger error boats from OTel error logs instead of HTTP 503

Worker upgraded from console app to web project (Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web)
and now hosts a /error-notification webhook endpoint. Monitored services
receive ASPIRE_MINECRAFT_ERROR_WEBHOOK env var pointing to the worker.

When 'Trigger Error' is clicked in the Aspire dashboard, the API service
emits ILogger.LogError (OTel error-level log) and posts to the worker's
webhook. The worker's ErrorBoatService spawns a creeper boat in the canal.

Health polling remains for weather/beacon effects (rain when service down).
Error boats are now a separate signal triggered by application errors.

- Worker .csproj: SDK changed to Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web
- Worker Program.cs: WebApplication.CreateBuilder + MapPost endpoint
- ErrorBoatService: extracted SpawnBoatCoreAsync, added SpawnBoatForErrorLogAsync
- MinecraftServerBuilderExtensions: WithAspireWorldDisplay adds HTTP endpoint,
  WithMonitoredResource sets ASPIRE_MINECRAFT_ERROR_WEBHOOK via annotation
- API service: trigger-error emits LogError + posts webhook notification

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Log session: boat junction redirect fix  Jeff tested ice canals, Rocket assigned to expose TrunkCanalX and redirect westbound boats at junction

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: redirect boats south at trunk canal junction

- Exposed TrunkCanalX from CanalService for boat routing
- MoveBoatsAsync now redirects westbound boats to southbound at trunk canal
  using data merge with Motion:[0,0,5] and "turned" tag to prevent re-redirect
- Boats slide west on branch canal ice  turn south at trunk  reach lake

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: kill-and-resummon boats at trunk canal junction

Minecraft physics overrides Motion set via 'data merge entity' on the
next game tick, so boats could never be redirected in-place. Instead,
we now kill arriving westbound boats and summon fresh southbound boats
with Motion:[0,0,5] at the same location. Freshly summoned entities
retain their initial Motion on blue_ice before physics takes over.

Also adds orphaned NoAI creeper cleanup in CleanupBoatsAsync.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: switch error visualization from boats to minecarts on rails

Boats cannot autonomously turn corners in vanilla Minecraft.
Minecarts on rails follow curved paths natively, including
turns at the trunk canal junction. Powered rails on blue_ice
provide propulsion. Creeper passengers ride through all curves.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: remove canal infrastructure, fix rail junction curves

Canals (excavation, walls, ice) are unnecessary for minecarts on rails.
Rails now placed directly on the ground surface (SurfaceY + 1).
Fixed junction curves by using regular rails at corners instead of
powered rails (which cannot curve). The lake landing zone is preserved.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(ai-team): merge decisions and log minecart rail session

* feat: replace shared trunk with per-row N-S lines and explicit curve junctions

The shared N-S trunk rail caused junction rails to have 3+ neighbors
(N, S from trunk + E from branch), preventing Minecraft from forming
L-curves. Minecarts crossed junctions instead of turning.

Fix: Each row of buildings gets its own independent N-S rail line at a
unique X position. Northernmost rows get the furthest-west lines so
E-W branches never cross other N-S lines. Curve rails are placed LAST
with explicit shape=south_east to force the westbound-to-southbound turn.

Removed: shared trunk, junction Z tracking, auto-curve reliance.
Added: per-row line assignment, 3-phase placement (E-W, N-S, curves).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: log health fix + minecart session

* fix: remove broken HealthCheckAnnotation.Key health path extraction

HealthCheckAnnotation.Key is a health check registration name, not a
URL path. Using it as HEALTH_PATH caused the worker to poll a non-existent
endpoint, making healthy resources appear unhealthy in Minecraft.

Without HEALTH_PATH, the worker checks the base URL which already returns
appropriate status codes for health detection.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: raise bridge clearance and preserve rails underneath

- Bridge deck raised from SurfaceY+2 to SurfaceY+4 (3-block clearance
  for minecart+creeper)
- Air clear starts at SurfaceY+2 to preserve rails at SurfaceY+1
- Bridge supports use oak_fence instead of stone_bricks (minecarts
  pass through fences)
- 3-step ramps (up from SurfaceY+1 to SurfaceY+4) replace 2-step

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: place E-W rail redstone torches adjacent to track (centerZ+1 not +2)

Redstone torches at centerZ+2 were too far to power the E-W powered
rails behind buildings. Changed to centerZ+1 (adjacent) to match the
N-S rail torch pattern which correctly uses lineX-1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: error minecart replay skipped due to early return on empty changes

SpawnBoatsForChangesAsync returned immediately when changes.Count == 0,
but the replay call from the worker loop passes Array.Empty to trigger
buffered _pendingChanges replay. The early return prevented buffered
error-triggered minecarts from ever spawning.

Fix: only return early when BOTH changes and _pendingChanges are empty.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add initial westward Motion to error minecart summon

Minecarts spawned stationary on flat powered rails because powered
rails on flat ground do NOT push stationary carts -- they only
accelerate existing motion. Added Motion:[-1.0d,0.0d,0.0d] NBT to
give the cart initial westward velocity toward the N-S trunk line.

Also added diagnostic logging throughout the spawn pipeline (resource
not found, cooldown active, caps reached, summon command) and webhook
failure logging in the API's trigger-error endpoint.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: update squad tooling and preserve minecart milestone

Minecarts are now spawning on error trigger and riding the rail
network through town! Updated squad templates and agent files
to v0.5.4 structure.

## Implementation Plan: Add Support for Configuration Value `allowed`
Field

### Understanding
- Z-Wave JS now supports `allowed` field in ValueMetadataNumeric for
defining specific allowed values (ranges with gaps)
- The `allowed` field is an array of `AllowedValue` objects: `{ value:
number } | { from: number, to: number, step?: number }`

### Changes Required

- [x] Explore codebase and understand current validation implementation
- [x] Add `allowed` field to `ZUIValueId` TypeScript type in backend
(`api/lib/ZwaveClient.ts`)
- [x] Update `_updateValueMetadata()` to pass `allowed` field from
Z-Wave JS metadata to frontend
- [x] Update `ValueId.vue` component to validate against `allowed`
values when present
- [x] Update validation logic to prioritize `allowed` over `min`/`max`
when both exist
- [x] Update error messages to reflect allowed values validation
- [x] Extract helper method to reduce code duplication (code review
feedback)
- [x] Add JSDoc documentation to new methods (code review feedback)
- [x] Fix validation for combobox inputs with `allowManualEntry` (issue
#4561 comment)
- [x] **Coerce value to number in `isValueAllowed()` and
`numberOutOfRange()` to handle string inputs from v-combobox** (PR
review feedback)
- [x] Run linting and tests (all pass)

### Files Modified
1. `api/lib/ZwaveClient.ts` - Added `allowed` field to `ZUIValueId` type
and pass it from metadata

coercion to handle string inputs from v-combobox

commit.message:
Parallelize build.py with multiprocessing.Pool(nproc) (#138)

* Initial plan

* Make build.py parallel with multiprocessing.Pool(nproc)

Use multiprocessing.Pool with os.cpu_count() workers to render posts,
collections, and pages in parallel. Each worker initializes its own
Liquid environment once via _init_worker, then _render_one handles
rendering and writing individual content items.

Benchmark (4 cores, 146 content items):
  Sequential: ~2.93s wall-clock
  Parallel:   ~1.98s wall-clock (1.48x speedup)

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Merge posts+collections into single parallel batch; drop unused collection .content writeback

Posts and collections had no interdependencies — the 3-batch sequential
ordering was happenstance. The only real dependency: feed.xml (a page)
reads post.content, so posts must finish before pages.

Profiled overhead (4 cores, 144 tasks):
  Pickling all tasks: ~23ms
  Pool creation+init: ~40ms
  Total overhead: ~63ms (2% of ~2900ms sequential build)

Benchmarks (median of 5, 4 cores):
  Sequential:          2.93s
  3-batch parallel:    2.01s
  2-batch parallel:    1.85s

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(engine): add deep watchdog and safety interlock tests (#178)

Add 57 comprehensive tests for watchdog and safety interlock subsystems:

- Concurrent SharedWatchdog access (thread-safe feed, arm/disarm, timeout visibility)
- Watchdog behavior during recovery sequences (timeout during fault, reset/rearm)
- Rapid state oscillation (fault/clear cycles, challenge/cancel cycles, estop/reset)
- Simultaneous/cascading faults (multi-fault logging, fault during challenge/ack)
- Timing guarantees (fault-to-zero <10ms, timeout response <1ms, estop <1ms)
- Challenge-response edge cases (wrong tokens, missing combo start, short hold, expiry)
- Safety state machine invariants (torque limits per state, hands-off timeout)
- Communication loss edge cases (no initial report, refresh prevents loss)
- Torque limit edge cases (zero max, safe mode limit)
- WatchdogTimeoutHandler edge cases (double timeout, reset/reuse)
- Property-based tests with proptest (200 cases per property):
  - Any fault yields zero torque
  - Early clear_fault rejected
  - clear_fault restores SafeTorque
  - Faulted blocks high-torque request
  - Watchdog timeout produces zero torque and fault
  - Torque bounded in normal operation
  - Emergency stop always zero
  - Clamp idempotent
  - Cancel challenge from SafeTorque fails

All tests return Result (no unwrap/expect per project policy).

fix(node): fix three bugs in Peers#cullExpiredNodesAndAddresses (#3346)

* fix(node): fix three bugs in Peers#cullExpiredNodesAndAddresses

Three bugs in the `#cullExpiredNodesAndAddresses` method of `Peers.ts`
prevented expired/orphaned peer nodes and stale addresses from ever being
cleaned up:

**Bug 1 – Early `return` when a commissioned node has exactly one address**
Inside the `for (const node of this)` loop, the shortcut path for
commissioned nodes with a single address used `return` instead of
`continue`.  A single such node anywhere in the peers map would
immediately exit the *entire* method, skipping all remaining peers.
This meant uncommissioned nodes that appeared after the first
single-address commissioned node in insertion order were never culled,
even after their TTL expired.

**Bug 2 – Early `return` in the "keep last address" branch**
The same `return`-instead-of-`continue` mistake in the branch that
preserves the freshest address when all addresses of a commissioned node
have expired.  Any commissioned node triggering this branch would silently
abort the loop and leave subsequent nodes un-processed.

**Bug 3 – Address update wrote back the original list**
After filtering expired addresses into `newAddresses`, the update call
passed `{ addresses }` (the *original* array captured at the top of the
loop body) instead of `{ addresses: newAddresses }`.  The result was a
no-op: the node's address list was never pruned.

Tests added in `ClientNodeTest.ts` ("orphaned peer cleanup" describe block):
- Verifies that an uncommissioned peer from an incomplete commissioning
  attempt persists across a controller restart (intentional: TTL hasn't
  expired yet, so the peer must survive for a potential retry).
- Verifies that an uncommissioned peer IS culled after its TTL expires
  even when a commissioned peer with one address is present (Bug 1/2
  regression test).
- Verifies that a stale address on a commissioned node is pruned on the
  next expiration tick (Bug 3 regression test).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

security: harden against path traversal, temp file prediction, TOCTOU, and DoS

Patches:
- disk_io: Replace predictable pid^nanos^attempt XOR temp-file naming with
  FNV-1a hash mixing PID, timestamp, thread ID, monotonic counter, and
  stack address (ASLR entropy). Prevents temp-file prediction attacks.
- disk_io: Replace exists() TOCTOU in next_merge_sidecar_path with
  OpenOptions::create_new() for atomic check-and-create.
- render: Block path-traversal in resolve_image_url() by rejecting URLs
  containing '..' path components (both / and \ separators).
- parse: Add MAX_PARSE_BYTES (64 MiB) input size limit to prevent OOM
  from enormous markdown documents. Truncates at last newline boundary.
- height: Clamp font_size to 1000px and wrap_width to 100K to prevent
  f32 overflow producing Infinity in height estimation.

Fuzz/adversarial tests added:
- Parser: null bytes, control chars, deep nesting, long lines, unclosed
  markers, enormous tables, oversized input truncation, mixed constructs.
- Renderer: path traversal URLs, safe URL resolution, adversarial URLs,
  dot-dot segment detection, extreme height estimation values.
- Formatter: adversarial inputs, CRLF multibyte preservation, fence edge
  cases.
- Merger: empty/binary/CRLF/unicode inputs, pre-existing conflict markers.
- Disk I/O: temp file suffix uniqueness, atomic sidecar creation, special
  filenames.

360 tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean.
